title: Sour Flaky Middle Eastern Olives Tagine
description: A bright, lowcalorie tagine starring pitted black olives, lemon, tomatoes, and chickpeas, finished with fresh herbs for a sour, flaky finish.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a heavy‑bottomed pot over medium heat.
Add the sliced red onion and garlic, sautéing until fragrant and translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in the diced tomatoes, cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper; cook for 5 minutes until the tomatoes start to soften.
Add the black olives and chickpeas, then pour in the lemon juice, reserving a tablespoon for garnish.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld.
Stir in the fresh parsley and lemon zest, adjusting seasoning with salt and pepper if needed.
Serve hot, spooning the aromatic mixture into a shallow bowl and drizzling the reserved lemon juice over the top.
Optionally, garnish with additional chopped parsley for a vibrant finish.
